title: Curriculum/Course Development and Review Standard
pillar: Strategic Pillar One: Teaching Learning and Student Success
supporting_units: Faculties, Departments, QAD, Makerere University
pathway: Needs Assessment → Concept development → Approval → Programme/course Development/Review → Approval by Academic Board → Verification and Validation by QAD → Submission to Mak/NCHE
purpose: This standard defines the process for the development and review of curriculum/course at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that curriculum/course developed meets the demand of industry and other stakeholders.
To ensure that curriculum/course reviewed is aligned with the demand of industry and other stakeholders.
To ensure the curriculum/course promotes holistic students’ growth.
To design curriculum/course that promotes equity and inclusiveness.
To enhance the employability and relevance of the graduates

performance_indicators: No. of programmes/course developed within approved timelines
% of programmes/course reviewed within approved timelines
No. of trainings conducted on curriculum/course development and review (CBET)
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Adherence to Approved NCHE Minimum standards for implementing competence-based education and training (CBET) in higher education institutions (2026) https://unche.or.ug/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Minimum-Standards-for-Implementing-Competence-Based-Education-and-Training.pdf Mak Quality Assurance Policy Framework https://policies.mak.ac.ug/sites/default/files/policies/Quality_Assurance_Policy_0.pdf
matrix_rows.process_text: Needs Assessment (2 months) → Concept development (1 month) → Approval (1 month) → Programme/courses Development/Review (4 months) → Approval by Academic Board (1 month) → Verification and Validation by QAD (1 month) → Submission to Mak/NCHE (2 months)
matrix_rows.frequency: Development as per schedule; Review as per NCHE guidelines
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Approved concept for programme/Courses development/review
matrix_rows.methodology: Online and physical, committees Stakeholder consultations, Benchmarking, Course review meetings, Compliance oversight by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: Graduate profiles and occupational profiling, stakeholders Facilities, Approved fees structure - Human resources

title: Student Academic Administration and Records Management Standard
pillar: Strategic Pillar One: Teaching Learning and Student Success
supporting_units: Faculties, Departments, QAD, Makerere University
pathway: Admissions → Registration → Timetabling → Examinations → Results Processing → Graduation
purpose: This standard defines the structured, transparent, and efficient academic administration services that support the student lifecycle at MUBS, ensuring compliance with NCHE and Makerere University standards, accuracy of records, and timely delivery of services.
objectives: To ensure fair, transparent, and merit-based student admissions processes.
To ensure accurate student registration and effective management of student records.
To provide efficient, accurate, and clash-free academic timetabling and scheduling services.
To ensure secure, credible, and timely administration of examinations and processing of results.
To coordinate orderly graduation processes and timely issuance of academic documents.
To maintain accurate student data, records, and institutional statistics for reporting and decision-making.
user_fee: Application fees, Functional fees, tuition fees as approved by NCHE and MUBS and Graduation fees

performance_indicators: % of admission applications processed within approved timelines
% of admitted students meeting the prescribed entry requirements
% accuracy of applicant data captured in the admissions system
% of admission lists approved and published before commencement of registration
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Adherence to NCHE guidelines https://unche.or.ug/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Minimum-Standards-for-Implementing-Competence-Based-Education-and-Training.pdf Mak. and MUBS Academic Policies Manual https://policies.mak.ac.ug/sites/default/files/policies/Makerere-Academic-Policies-Manual-Revised-Version-2025.pdf
matrix_rows.process_text: Approved intake figures (Before Advert) → Advertisement Preparation and Approval (1 week) → Receipt of Applications (as advertised) → Verification of Applications (3 weeks) → Pre-entry Tests/Interviews, where applicable (as scheduled) →Admissions Board Recommendation and Academic Board/Senate Approval (4 weeks) → Release of Admission Lists (3 working days) → Issuance of Admission Letters (1 week) → Change of programme (Apply within 2 weeks of reporting).
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Application fee
matrix_rows.methodology: Online and physical, committees meetings, Admission tests, Compliance Oversight by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: Approved intake figures, -Approved accredited programmes, -Facilities, -Approved fees structure -Active online system, -website and internet

performance_indicators: No. of clash-free timetables published per semester within required deadline
No. of timetables disseminated across access channels
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Zero clashes for students’ classes and venues; Optimal utilisation of lecture halls. Conformance to QA Policy and Guidelines, Mak. and MUBS Academic Policies and Guidelines.
matrix_rows.process_text: Collection of Course Offerings and staff Availability (2 weeks) → Data Entry and Clash-Free Scheduling (1 Week) → Consultation with Deans/HODs (1 week) → Preparation for Teaching Timetable Meetings (2 Weeks) → Final Approval and Publication of Timetables (1 Week) → Teaching Timetable Published (2 weeks before semester) → Coursework Timetable Published (1 week before coursework period) → Examination Timetable Published (2 weeks before examination period).
matrix_rows.coverage: All scheduled academic programmes, courses, and examination papers for the semester/ Module.
matrix_rows.frequency: Twice per semester (Teaching timetable and Examination timetable)
matrix_rows.access_criteria: -Published on Departmental noticeboards and email accounts -Via ACMIS and MUBSEP
matrix_rows.methodology: -Accredited programme structures -Staff list -Faculty/ Campus teaching loads -Venue lists with teaching and assessment capacities -Timetabling software -Internal Examiners lists -Persons of Disability lists -Compliance checks by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: All staff and registered students

performance_indicators: % of newly admitted students attending orientation programmes
% of orientation activities conducted according to the approved schedule
% of student satisfaction with orientation services and information provided
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Adherence to MaK and MUBS Academic policies and guidelines.
matrix_rows.process_text: Draft and Approve Orientation Programme (2 weeks before reporting) → Publish and Communicate Orientation Schedule (1 week before reporting) → General Welcome Assembly (Day 1) → Faculty/Departmental Briefings and Campus Tours (Days 2–3) → Health, Security, Library, ICT, and Student Support Inductions (Days 4–5) → Post-Orientation Feedback Survey (within 3 days after completion).
matrix_rows.coverage: All newly admitted students across all campuses.
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Fully admitted student with a valid admission letter to MUBS programmes
matrix_rows.methodology: Centralised assemblies, departmental break-out sessions, physical campus tours, peer- mentorship groups, online portal walk-through, Compliance oversight by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: - Approved orientation budget - Orientation program booklets and guides - Venue and public address systems - Staff, and student leaders - ICT infrastructure and e-learning training materials

matrix_rows.service_description: Registration of Students for MUBS programmes
performance_indicators: No. of students registered Registration completion rate % of students registered within the approved registration period
% accuracy of student registration records captured in the system
Aver. turnaround time for processing student registration
No. of registration complaints or errors resolved within the prescribed timelines
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Accuracy of student data in the system (ACMIS); Adherence to the Approved Mak and MUBS Academic Policies and Regulation.
matrix_rows.process_text: Upload Biodata for New Entrants (1 week) → Verify Biodata and Academic Documents (4 weeks) → Activate Registration Portal for Continuing Students (1 week after release of results) → Course Selection and Approval of Electives/Options (where applicable) → Process Withdrawal, Reinstatement, Stay-Put, and Other Academic Status Cases (throughout the semester) → Issue Registration Permits (immediately upon completion) → Requisition new identity cards (1 week) →Compilation of disaggregated Statistical data and report (Within two weeks).
matrix_rows.coverage: All continuing and fresh students enrolled in the academic semester.
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Paid required tuition fees, functional fees and NCHE fees; Proof of admission for new entrants; Academic documents for new entrants; Cleared previous semester debts for continuing; Results for previous semester and no active academic suspension.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical for new entrants; Online Self-Service (ACMIS portal); Physical walk-in for appeals/special cases, Compliance oversight by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: Functional Student Information System (ACMIS); Approved Admission Lists; Registration policies and procedures; Registration officials; ICT Infrastructure (Computers, Internet Bandwidth); IT Support Staff; Appropriate venue with ICT facilities

matrix_rows.service_description: Moderation of Formative Assessment Question papers
performance_indicators: % of question papers moderated on time
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Compliance with NCHE Minimum Standards for CBET; Mak and MUBS Academic Policies and Guidelines; QA Policy and Guidelines; and adherence to Approved Formative Assessment Timetable.
matrix_rows.process_text: Approved Formative Assessment timetable (4 Weeks before commencement) → Approved course outline (1 week of the semester) → submission of draft question to HOD by Internal Examiner (4 weeks) →Verification of adherence of the approved guidelines (1 week before commencement of exams) → Approved moderation submission form (2 days) → Submission to the production Unit (Immediate).

matrix_rows.methodology: Physical Monitoring by HODs & Deans Compliance oversight by QAD

matrix_rows.process_text: Invitation of EE by HODs (6 months before commencement of Academic Year) → Confirmation availability by EE (Beginning of Semester) → Requests and approval of logistical requirements (Within 4 Weeks) → Examining (1-2 Weeks) → Meeting Faculty staff (End of examining period) → Submission of Reports (Immediate).

title: Course Instruction and Delivery Standard
owner_label: Faculty Deans and Heads of Academic Departments
pillar: Strategic Pillar One: Teaching Learning and Student Success
supporting_units: Quality Assurance Directorate (QAD), Office of the School Registrar, MIS, E-Learning Unit, Industry Supervisors
pathway: Approved Course Outline aligned to CLOs→ Lecture delivered on schedule with active methods → Recording of Attendance → Uploading Learning Materials (where applicable) → Monitoring, Evaluation and Feedback.
purpose: This standard presents the instruction and delivery of courses.
objectives: To ensure that staff adopt the appropriate instructions and delivery approaches.
To ensure that instructions and delivery conform to Competency Based Education and Training (CBET).
To conduct course and staff evaluations using SECAT and other approved assessment tools.
To ensure that all the topics in the approved course outline are covered as per the timetable.
To monitor, evaluate and receive feedback on the instructions and delivery of courses for continuous improvement.
user_fee: No direct user fees; service is part of tuition and functional fees.

matrix_rows.service_description: Appointment of internal and external examiners (IE & EE)
performance_indicators: i) 100% appointed internal and external examiners
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Conformance to NCHE External Examining Framework; Mak and MUBS QA Policies and Guidelines.
matrix_rows.process_text: Departmental sourcing, verification and recommendations of IE &EE to Faculty Board (6 months before beginning of AY) →Faculty Board recommendations of IE & EE to AB (4 weeks) → Appointments of IE & EE) (Within 4 weeks) → Report to management (Immediate).
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Qualified Staff at the level of lecturer and above (IE), For EE, Senior Lecturer and above
matrix_rows.methodology: Sourcing, Verification, Meetings, Appointments, Monitoring by Deans, Compliance oversight by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: Approved programmes, Qualified staff, Qualified Candidates for EE, Workloads, Financial Resources.

performance_indicators: i) % of course, outlines aligned to CLOs format

ii) % of courses outlines uploaded by week one iii) % of students accessing the course outlines by week one iv) % of scheduled class sessions delivered v)Students’ Satisfaction rate on course instruction and delivery v)compliance rate on instruction and delivery approaches to CBET vi) % of course, attendance vii)% of courses monitored and evaluated
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Conformance to National Council for Higher Education (NCHE) Minimum Standard; ODeL Policy; Mak and MUBS QA Policies and Guidelines.
matrix_rows.process_text: Appointed Internal Examiner (4 weeks before commencement of semester) →Develop/Update Course Outline (Before beginning of semester) → Lecture instruction and delivery (As per the teaching timetable) → Recording of attendance (Per lecture) → Uploading materials (Per lecture) → Monitoring, Evaluation and Feedback (Weekly) → Report to HOD (As necessary).
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Scheduled Notional hours delivered in full, Active engagement approaches used as scheduled, Digital learning component integrated, Attendance recorded and reported
matrix_rows.methodology: Face to Face, Online Facilitation, Teaching plan uploaded, conducting sessions and recording, Compliance checks by Faculty/departmental QA committees, Compliance oversight by QAD
matrix_rows.inputs: Learners, Approved course outlines, Qualified and competent staff, Technology Infrastructure, Deans, HODs and Programme Lead, Registry

title: Stores & Asset Registry Service Delivery Standards
supporting_units: Procurement & Disposal Unit (PDU), Estates & Works Department, ICT Directorate, Internal Audit, Board of Survey, User Departments
pathway: Procurement → Delivery & Technical Acceptance → Stores Custody → Asset Tagging → Register Updating → Verification & Reconciliation → Reporting & Disposal
purpose: This standard defines the structured, transparent, and accountable processes for the receipt, custody, tagging, registration, verification, and reconciliation of institutional assets at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure accurate and timely receipt, inspection, and technical acceptance of all institutional assets.
To maintain secure, traceable, and well‑controlled custody of all assets under Stores management, ensuring zero loss, theft, or undocumented movement.
To ensure all MUBS assets are uniquely tagged, classified, and linked to the Electronic Asset Register.
To maintain a complete, accurate, and up‑to‑date MUBS and IFMS Asset Register.
To conduct systematic physical verification, reconciliation, and variance reporting across all campuses.
To support transparent and compliant asset disposal processes through accurate records, Board of Survey inputs, and timely updates to the register.

To identify, verify, and award scholarships to eligible students, including academic achievers, persons with disabilities, and sports persons, through a structured and accountable process.

To ensure timely, accessible, and consistent communication with scholarship applicants and beneficiaries across all platforms.

purpose: The Student Welfare, Leadership and Sports standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for administering student welfare services at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure the timely, accurate, and secure disbursement of living-out allowances for food and accommodation to registered government-sponsored students.

To facilitate transparent, democratic, and constitutionally compliant student leadership structures through the systematic coordination, budgeting, and execution of annual Guild elections and cabinet selections.

To cultivate athletic excellence, physical wellness, and competitive sports development across all approved university programs by deploying qualified coaches and maintaining strict compliance with National Council of Sports standards.

To champion diversity and equity in MUBS sports by actively collaborating with the Disability Resource and Learning Centre to maximize the participation rate of students with disabilities in institutional tournaments and national leagues.

To enforce rigorous administrative controls and document verification protocols to validate student registration, financial eligibility, and academic progress before granting access to welfare allowances or leadership positions.

title: Medical Support and Counselling Services Standard
supporting_units: Dean of Students’ Office, Human Resource Directorate, Disability Resource & Learning Centre
pathway: Triage → Consultation → Diagnosis → Treatment → Counselling/Referral → Follow-Up → Reporting
purpose: The Medical Support and Counselling Services Standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for delivering timely, professional, and inclusive health and psychosocial services to students and staff at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all patients receive prompt triage, consultation, diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up care.
To provide accessible, confidential, and responsive counselling services addressing mental, social, and emotional needs.
To ensure that emergency cases are promptly prioritised, managed, and reported to the relevant authorities.
To promote holistic wellbeing that supports academic success and staff productivity through integrated medical and counselling services.
To maintain accurate medical and counselling records, referrals, and reports to ensure continuity of care, compliance, and effective institutional planning.

performance_indicators: % of presenting patients who undergo triage and initial clinical assessment within the target wait-time threshold
Average Laboratory Turnaround Time (TAT) from sample collection to verified result release
% of prescription forms dispensed accurately without stock-out or pharmaceutical delays
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% non-discriminatory, equitable, and patient-centered care in adherence with Occupational Safety And Health Act https://media.ulii.org/media/legislation/18557/source_file/dab977077c1cf6aa/2006-9.pdf Human Resource Manual 2009 (As Ammended In 2024) https://hrms.mubs.ac.ug/hr-docs/view2/1 .
matrix_rows.process_text: Conduct triage and initial assessment in 10 minutes→ Provide doctor’s consultation in 15 minutes → Carry out laboratory tests and/or scans in 20 minutes → Complete diagnosis and initiate treatment plan in10 minutes → Recommend counselling or make a referral as required → Provide nursing care and support as required → Formulate clinical referral notes for tertiary medical facilities when required → Dispense prescribed medication at the pharmacy 5 minutes
matrix_rows.coverage: All valid university staff, registered students, and immediate cleared dependents across all campuses.
matrix_rows.frequency: Operational continuously on a Daily basis (24/7 for emergency and inpatient triage).
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: MUBS Staff, Registered Students, and Authorized Dependents
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Presentation of a valid MUBS Student ID, Staff ID, or dependent medical scheme profile card. - Physical presence at the triage deck with active clinical symptoms or medical requests.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical (One on one),
matrix_rows.inputs: Certified medical practitioners, nurses, and laboratory technologists. -Fully equipped laboratory diagnostic machines and reagents. -Essential Medicines and Health Supplies (EMHS) pharmacy stock. -Integrated Clinic Management System -Approved institutional medical operating budget.

matrix_rows.service_description: Counselling services received by students and staff
performance_indicators: i) No. of students and staff accessing counselling services within a defined reporting period
ii) Aver. waiting time before clients receive counselling support after booking or referral
iii) % of counselling cases successfully managed and closed within the stipulated timeframe
iv) Rate of client satisfaction with counselling services provided

To provide confidential, professional, and accessible counselling services to staff and their families.
To ensure the prompt and compassionate processing of death benefits to support bereaved families.
To administer wedding (nuptial) support fairly and in accordance with the Human Resource Manual.
To ensure accurate and timely processing of tuition waiver and related educational benefits for staff biological children.
To ensure accurate remittance and annual reporting of retirement benefits and statutory contributions (MURBS/NSSF).

title: Security and Safety Services Standard
supporting_units: Estates & Works, Dean of Students’ Office, Health Services Centre, ICT Unit
pathway: Campus Security → Patrols → Emergency Response → Incident Management → Reporting
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for ensuring a safe, secure, and supportive environment for students, staff, and visitors at MUBS.
objectives: To conduct regular campus patrols to deter crime and enhance institutional safety.
To ensure prompt, professional, and effective response to all emergency incidents.
To manage lost and found items securely, transparently, and efficiently.
To provide security services that support a safe environment for teaching, learning, research, and campus life.

performance_indicators: % of daily scheduled campus patrols successfully completed as per the master security plan
% of designated patrol checkpoints and routes verified via electronic guard-tour systems No. of safety hazards, unauthorized breaches, and security incidents proactively detected

matrix_rows.process_text: -mapping of deployable areas to ascertain the actual of security guards. (immediate) → mobilize the required number of security guards (immediate) → Give guidelines for the activity (after mobilization) → Supervise the activity (as per schedule) → Receive reports from the guards → Debrief to security guards (at the end of the activity)
matrix_rows.coverage: All perimeter borders, parking lots of MUBS.
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Unrestricted requirement for safety presence across public campus spaces.
matrix_rows.methodology: site survey before deployment of security guards De-brief
matrix_rows.inputs: Safety gears Torches Reports from security guards Staff

performance_indicators: Average time elapsed from the initial call or alarm receipt to physical arrival on the scene
% of critical priority incidents responded to within the mandatory 5-minute threshold
% of localized incidents safely contained and resolved without requiring external law enforcement
No. of incidents escalated due to resource gaps or communication delays
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% detection of unauthorised gadgets and adherence to Occupational Safety And Health Act https://media.ulii.org/media/legislation/18557/source_file/dab977077c1cf6aa/2006-9.pdf
matrix_rows.process_text: Receive emergency call or alarm and record incident details → Triage incident by threat severity level → Locate closest unit and dispatch within 2 minutes → Units arrive on scene within 5 minutes and secure perimeter → Mitigate threat, apply first aid, and log incident resolution.

title: Disability Inclusion and Resource Mobilization Standard
supporting_units: Human Resource Directorate, Office of the School Bursar, Office of the Dean of Students, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, Estates and Facilities Directorate, ICT Directorate, External Disabled Persons Organizations (DPOs)
pathway: Needs Assessment → Stakeholder Engagement → Intervention Design → Resource Preparation → Implementation → Monitoring & Evaluation
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, operational processes, and quality benchmarks for delivering disability advocacy, inclusive training, and strategic resource sourcing initiatives at MUBS.
objectives: To foster an empathetic, inclusive, and non-discriminatory institutional culture across all MUBS administrative and academic departments.
To equip academic faculty with functional capabilities in inclusive teaching, testing, and assessment methodologies for learners with diverse functional needs.
To bridge communication gaps across campuses by delivering structured Ugandan Sign Language training workshops to frontline staff and student leaders.
To systematically map, track, and target global and domestic donor funding opportunities matching the school's inclusive development roadmap.
To build strategic consortia and joint research partnerships with grant-experienced organizations and international academia.
To secure external development capital specifically targeted at high-tech assistive technologies, physical infrastructure retrofits, and digital portal accessibility upgrades.

performance_indicators: No. Of disability awareness campaigns conducted No. Of participants trained/ sensitized

matrix_rows.process_text: Conduct needs assessments to identify awareness gaps and plan interventions (Annually) → Identify and engage key stakeholders (1 month) → Develop tailored, inclusive content (1 month) → Mobilize required resources (2 months) → Implement awareness activities (1 week) → Conduct monitoring, evaluation, and submit reports to management (1 week).
matrix_rows.coverage: -MUBS and its Regional Campuses. -Scope includes sign language training, inclusive teaching/assessment methods for students with disabilities, sensitization sessions, and continuous digital awareness campaigns.
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: Differently abled students, staff, management, Council, and general stakeholders
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Open to all MUBS students, staff, and interested institutional stakeholders.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: In-person training sessions, workshops, physical sensitization seminars, and outreach campaigns Online: Continuous digital awareness campaigns via email, intranet, institutional social media, and virtual webinars
matrix_rows.inputs: -Financial resources -List of approved students and staff with disabilities. -Accessible training spaces. -Qualified trainers and facilitators. -Information, Education, and Communication (IEC) materials. -Established partnerships and advocacy networks.

title: International Student Services and Internationalisation Standard
supporting_units: Office of the School Registrar, School Bursar’s Office, MIS, eLearning
pathway: Admission/Engagement → Pre-arrival Preparation → Immigration & Travel Coordination → Arrival & Reception → Registration → Orientation → Integration → Continuous Support → Monitoring → Reporting → Learning
purpose: This standard defines the minimum service expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for attracting, onboarding, integrating, and supporting international students, visiting scholars, and other international academic participants at MUBS.
objectives: To attract, support admission, onboarding, and registration of international students within prescribed MUBS timelines.

To facilitate full compliance with immigration, visa, and travel requirements for international students, visiting scholars, and academic staff through coordinated institutional support and liaison with relevant government authorities.

To provide structured pre-arrival, arrival, and post-arrival support services, including accommodation facilitation, documentation verification, registration, and issuance of institutional identification and access credentials.
To promote effective academic, social, and cultural integration of international students into the MUBS community through structured orientation programmes, mentorship, and engagement in campus life.
To ensure continuous monitoring and support of international students’ academic progress, welfare, and adjustment, including timely referral of identified challenges to relevant support units.

To strengthen MUBS Internationalisation by facilitating student and staff mobility programmes, international partnerships, exchange programmes, visiting scholar engagement, and global academic collaboration.

performance_indicators: No of international students onboarded and registered on time
Satisfaction rate of international students Percentage of international students successfully integrated into MUBS social and support networks within their first semester
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% error-free rate on all government immigration portal submissions.
matrix_rows.process_text: Compile information on admitted international students within 3 working days of receiving admission lists → Review and verify beneficiary documentation for immigration support within 2 working days → Coordinate visa and immigration support with the relevant government offices → Facilitate accommodation arrangements and other essential pre-arrival requirements, where applicable, within 1 week → Coordinate arrival logistics with relevant School offices and provide reception and transition support upon arrival → Verify original travel and immigration documents upon arrival → Coordinate issuance of MUBS identification cards and other institutional documentation within 2 working days of registration → Conduct orientation on MUBS policies, academic systems, student support services, and campus life within 4 working days of arrival→ Monitor students throughout the transition period and promptly identify and refer academic, financial, immigration, welfare, or psychosocial concerns to the appropriate support units → Transition students into the standard MUBS student support systems and submit periodic onboarding reports to Management.
matrix_rows.coverage: Visa applications, accommodation, bank account set ups, social-cultural assimilation.
matrix_rows.frequency: Continuous delivery at least 30 days prior to start of the semester and continuous throughout the semester.
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: International professors, post-doctoral fellows, international full time undergraduate and post graduate students/ potential students, staff transferred to support MUBS, exchange students
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Individuals duly admitted, recruited, nominated, or otherwise authorised by the University for international engagement.
matrix_rows.methodology: Online and physical, clearance, engagement, concierge transition, reception, pairing, monitoring, compilation, reporting,
matrix_rows.inputs: Competent staff, integrated information systems, immigration support documentation, welcome and reception facilities, office space, transport support (where applicable), communication facilities, and partnerships with relevant internal and external stakeholders.

title: Strategic Planning, Work Planning and Monitoring & Evaluation Standard

purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for preparing institutional plans, aligning budgets, extracting procurement plans, and monitoring implementation at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all plans and budgets are aligned with the MUBS Strategic Plan.
To coordinate the consolidation, review, and timely approval of annual and quarterly work plans.
To facilitate the preparation and submission of procurement plans derived from approved work plans to the Procurement and Disposal Unit (PDU) for implementation.
To strengthen monitoring and evaluation processes for tracking performance, assessing results, and supporting evidence-based decision-making.
To ensure timely submission of reports to Management and relevant committees in order to enhance accountability and continuous improvement.

title: Procurement, Contract Management and Disposal Standard
pillar: Human Capital, Governance and Institutional Sustainability
supporting_units: Office of the School Bursar, Internal Audit, User Departments, Contracts Committee, PPDA
pathway: Procurement Planning → Solicitation → Evaluation → Contract Award → Contract Management → Supplier Evaluation → Disposal → Records Management
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for planning, soliciting, evaluating, awarding, managing, and closing procurement and disposal activities at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all procurement activities comply with the PPDA Act, related regulations, and institutional policies.
To promote timely and participatory procurement planning aligned with approved budgets and institutional priorities.
To ensure transparency, fairness, and competitiveness in bid solicitation, evaluation, and contract award processes.
To strengthen contract management in order to ensure timely delivery, quality assurance, and value for money.
To establish objective supplier performance evaluation mechanisms to support informed future procurement decisions.
To ensure transparent, timely, and compliant disposal of institutional assets.
To maintain secure, well-indexed, and easily retrievable procurement records for audit and compliance purposes.

title: Financial Management, Accounting and Revenue Administration Standard
pillar: Human Capital, Governance and Institutional Sustainability
supporting_units: Internal Audit, Human Resource Directorate, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, ICT Unit, All units.
pathway: Billing → Budgeting → Reporting → Payroll → Compliance → Revenue Management
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing financial transactions, budgeting, reporting, payroll, compliance, and revenue collection at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure accurate and timely tuition billing and receipting in alignment with student registration records.
To facilitate budget preparation and execution in accordance with approved timelines, institutional priorities, and national guidelines.
To ensure the preparation and timely submission of accurate and compliant financial reports.
To process payroll accurately, confidentially, and within the stipulated payment timelines.
To address audit and compliance queries promptly, transparently, and effectively.
To ensure that all collected revenue is properly accounted for, reconciled, and reported.

matrix_rows.methodology: Billing, receipting

matrix_rows.process_text: Draft and Issue budget call circular (March) →Guide units on submission requirements →Compile unit submissions (By 15 April) → Align consolidated budget with MUBS priorities (15–25 April) →Submit final budget (By 30 April) →Track execution against approved allocations (throughout the financial year)

title: Staff Performance Management and Development Standard
pillar: Human Capital, Governance and Institutional Sustainability
supporting_units: Office of the School Secretary, All Units, Top Management, Appointments Board, MUBS council
pathway: Onboarding → Target Setting → Performance Monitoring → Appraisal → Workforce Assessment → Leave Management → Staff Development → Promotion
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing staff performance, growth, and progression at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all newly recruited and appointed staff are effectively onboarded and integrated into their respective roles.
To establish, monitor, and appraise performance targets in a structured, transparent, and objective manner.
To conduct fair and timely workforce assessments to support promotions, confirmations, and career progression.
To manage staff, leave, in a compliant, accurate, and efficient manner.
To align staff development initiatives with institutional needs, quality assurance standards, and individual career aspirations.
To ensure that staff promotions are conducted through merit-based, transparent, and timely procedures.

performance_indicators: % of newly recruited staff successfully on boarded within the stipulated timeframe
Completion rate of induction/orientation programmes for new staff
New staff satisfaction rate with the onboarding process
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Conforms Human Resource Manual 2009 (As Ammended In 2024) https://hrms.mubs.ac.ug/hr-docs/view2/1
matrix_rows.process_text: HR Directorate (HRD) interfaces with the new hire, collects primary biodata, and issues critical HR files (Within 5 working days of contract acceptance) →Notify the respective Head of Department (HOD) of the new hire's reporting date (Within 5 working days) → Formal introduction to the HOD, department team, and assignment of role expectations/academic workflows (Within 5 working days) →HOD submits written confirmation of the employee's Assumption of Duty to HRD (Within 5 working days) →Set up official corporate email, build the HRMS portal profile, and capture banking/tax data for payroll entry (Within 5 working days) → HOD assesses role clarity, institutional cultural fit, and early training needs (Within 3 months) →Conduct initial probation performance review and deliver feedback to the hire (Within 3 months) →Submit a consolidated onboarding cycle report to Top Management (Within 3 months post-cycle).
matrix_rows.coverage: All newly recruited academic, administrative, and support staff across all campuses.
matrix_rows.frequency: Executed continuously upon hire, with formal aggregate inductions conducted Twice a Year.
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: Newly Recruited Academic and Administrative Staff
matrix_rows.access_criteria: -Issuance and formal acceptance of a valid MUBS employment contract. -Physical reporting for duty at the assigned station.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: Face-to-face departmental introductions, physical campus tours, one-on-one mentor pairing, and interactive peer-networking workshops. Online/Digital: Digital provisioning of IT accounts, mandatory virtual compliance modules on the intranet, and online employee profile registration.
matrix_rows.inputs: -MUBS HR Manual 2009 (As amended). -Standard onboarding checklist and welcome documentation pack. -Secure HRMS portal login credentials. -Departmental role profiles and KPI template

matrix_rows.service_description: Onboarding newly appointed Deans of Faculties, Campus Chiefs and Heads of Departments
performance_indicators: I)No of staff in new positions onboarded Ii)Average time taken to onboard newly appointed Deans of Faculties, Campus Chiefs and Heads of Departments
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Conformity to Human Resource Manual 2009 (As Ammended In 2024) https://hrms.mubs.ac.ug/hr-docs/view2/1
matrix_rows.process_text: Newly appointed Dean of Faculty, Campus, and Head of Department approval hierarchies set on the HR portal (Within 5 working days) →Remuneration adjustments on the Allowances payroll made (Within 1 month) →HRD organises orientation sessions for the newly appointed Deans of Faculties, Campus Chiefs and Heads of Departments and issues them a copy of the HR Manual (Within 2 months after acceptance) →Deans of Faculties, Campus Chiefs and Heads of Departments assessed and given feedback on performance on set targets (Within 1 year) →Report to Management on the onboarding process Within 3 months after process)
matrix_rows.coverage: All new Deans of Faculties, Campus Chiefs and Heads of Departments
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: Newly Appointed Faculty Deans, Regional Campus Chiefs, and Heads of Departments
matrix_rows.access_criteria: -Formal instrument of appointment or election letter signed by the Appointing Authority/Council. -Signed formal handover report from the outgoing office holder.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: Executive peer-to-peer mentoring sessions, strategic handover board meetings, leadership retreats, and physical distribution of institutional policy briefs. Online/Digital: Advanced system administration training, configuration of electronic approval keys, and digital leadership repository access provisioning.
matrix_rows.inputs: -Valid letters of appointment to leadership roles. -Outgoing officer handover reports. -Master institutional policies, strategic plan, and budget guidelines. -Updated HRMS administrative workflow matrices

performance_indicators: I)%of staff appraised Ii)% of exceptional performers offered advancement opportunities Iii)No. of staff placed on performance improvement plans
matrix_rows.quality_standard: As per the approved Human Resource Manual 2009 (As Ammended In 2024) https://hrms.mubs.ac.ug/hr-docs/view2/1
matrix_rows.process_text: Set performance targets in 3 months →Performance implementation and monitoring in 1 year →Performance appraisal in 1 month →Presentation of performance outcome recommendations to Top Management for 2 weeks →Presentation of Management’s recommendations on the consolidated performance appraisal exercise in 2 weeks →Performance improvement, rewards and sanctions
matrix_rows.access_criteria: All fulltime staff
matrix_rows.methodology: Face to face, meetings, Online
matrix_rows.inputs: Approved appraisal forms, Qualified staff, System, Internet, Logistical resources.

matrix_rows.service_description: Coordination of the MUBS Performance Management Process
performance_indicators: % of eligible academic and administrative staff successfully appraised within the active financial year
% of exceptional performers formally recommended for career advancement or recognition opportunities
Number of underperforming staff placed on structured Performance Improvement Plans (PIPs)

matrix_rows.process_text: Unit Heads and staff agree on definitive performance targets at the start of the appraisal cycle (Within the first 3 months) →Execute daily work monitoring and maintain ongoing performance evidence logs (Over a 1-year window) →Conduct formal year-end appraisal assessments, scoring against the signed target agreements (Completed within 1 month) →Compile and present performance outcome files to Top Management for initial consideration (Within 2 weeks) →Present management's consolidated recommendations and appraisal insights to Council (Within 2 weeks) →Apply approved recommendations, including performance rewards, development tracking, or PIP sanctions.

matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: Face-to-face appraisal review meetings, classroom teaching evaluations, target defense panels, and physical performance log verification. Online/Digital: Electronic self-appraisal submissions via the HR portal, digital KPI scorecard tracking, and online routing of supervisor marks.
matrix_rows.inputs: -Approved MUBS HR Manual 2024. -Standardized institutional performance appraisal forms. -Signed individual performance target agreements. - Departmental baseline output tracking records.

performance_indicators: I)% of leave applications processed on time Ii)Accuracy of leave balances

matrix_rows.service_description: Staff engaged in Continuous Professional Development (CPD)

matrix_rows.quality_standard: As per MUBS HR Manual, MUBS Quality Assurance Policy and Guidelines.
matrix_rows.process_text: Conduct a Training Needs Assessment -12 months →Compile Departmental Plans and plan for delivery, 6 months →Receive applications from staff for long and short-term trainings) (As and when admissions to study programmes are received) →Table applications at the STADeC Committee in 4 weeks after assessment →Present STADeC recommendations to the Top Management Committee 36 weeks →Communicate Management resolutions on staff requests-1 week after Committee meeting → -Receive progress reports from staff, 6 months →Present consolidated Study Progress report to Management for consideration ,12 months →Carry out monitoring and Evaluation, 6 months →Carry out short term trainings according to Departmental plans in 36 weeks

matrix_rows.process_text: Identification of the need by user department in line with establishment (2 weeks) →Applications considered at Department (at Staff Selections and Promotions Committee) and Faculty level (at Faculty Staff Selection and Promotions Committee (1 month) →Verification (1 week) →Submit to appointments and Promotions Committee for senior lecturer and lecturer level (1 month) →Submit to Top Management Committee consideration (1 month) →Submit to Appointments Board decision (1 month) →Communicate Appointments Board decision to beneficiaries within 1 week)
matrix_rows.coverage: Staff at level of lecturer seeking for promotion to senior lecturer
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Departmental Meetings, Committee Meetings, Evaluations, Performance Appraisals, Internal Adverts, Faculty/ Departmental websites Appointments Board Meeting Letters, Email communication
matrix_rows.methodology: -MUBS HR Manual 2024 -Approved staff establishment structure. -Staff application letter -certified documents. -Performance appraisals. -Committee minutes -budget

performance_indicators: Percentage of approved promotion requests successfully concluded within the established staffing structure
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Promotions shall comply with the MUBS Human Resource Manual (Amended 2024), approved establishment structure, and applicable labour regulations.
matrix_rows.process_text: Identification of promotion needs by user departments in line with approved establishment (2 weeks) → Submission and consolidation of applications for Senior Administrative positions at departmental/faculty/campus level (1 month) → Consideration of other promotions through Annual Staff Performance Evaluation Exercise (1 month) → Promotions to Deputy Chief positions considered by Management and Appointments Board (2 months) → Verification by Principal and Human Resource Directorate (1 week) → Review by Interviewing Committee (1 month) → Consideration by Top Management Committee (1 month) → Approval by Appointments Board (1 month) → Communication of outcomes by Human Resource Directorate (1 week)
matrix_rows.coverage: All eligible non-academic staff of MUBS
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Must be confirmed staff within approved establishment with satisfactory performance appraisals and eligibility as per HR Manual
matrix_rows.methodology: Departmental needs assessment, Internal advertisement/publication of vacancies, Application submission by eligible staff, Performance appraisal records, Structured interviews and evaluations, Committee deliberations and approvals, Appointments Board decision-making process, Official communication of outcomes
matrix_rows.inputs: Approved establishment structure Staff applications and CVs Certified academic and professional documents Performance appraisal reports Committee minutes Budget provisions for salary adjustments

title: Internal Audit and Assurance Services Standard
pillar: Human Capital, Governance and Institutional Sustainability
supporting_units: Office of the School Bursar, Strategy & Projects Unit, All units, Internal Audit Directorate.
pathway: Annual Planning → Quarterly Audit Engagements → Reporting → Follow-Up → Liaison with External Auditors Standard Owner: Internal Audit Directorate
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for conducting independent, objective, and risk-based audits at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that internal audit engagements are aligned with the MUBS Strategic Plan and the approved annual audit work plan.
To conduct audit processes in compliance with national and international auditing standards, including the Internal Auditor General’s guidelines.
To produce audit findings and reports that enhance transparency, accountability, and value for money in institutional operations.
To promote the implementation of audit recommendations to strengthen internal controls and governance systems.
To ensure timely submission of quarterly and annual audit reports to the Internal Auditor General, MoFPED, and the Audit and Risk Management Committee in line with statutory requirements.
To conduct follow-up audits to monitor implementation of recommendations and promote continuous improvement.

matrix_rows.service_description: Annual auditing and submission of reports to the Internal Auditor General
performance_indicators: i) Number of statutory quarterly audit reports compiled and submitted on schedule
ii) % of internal audit recommendations formally adopted and implemented by Management
iii) Average timeline for escalating high-priority systemic governance risks to Council
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% compliance with Public Finance Management Act https://www.finance.go.ug/sites/default/files/2023-10/Uganda%20Public%20Finance%20Management%20Act%202015.pdf International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing (IIA Standards), and the Local Governments Financial and Accounting Regulations.
matrix_rows.process_text: Draft the risk-based annual audit workplan aligned to the approved MUBS strategic plan and budget (Within 4 weeks) →Forward the annual workplan to the Audit and Risk Management Committee of Council (2 weeks prior to meeting) →Submit the approved Annual Workplan to the Internal Auditor General, copying the Accounting Officer (By April) →Plan quarterly engagements defining scope (Financial, compliance, value for money, operational, environmental, performance) and allocate field auditors (3 days) →Issue formal information requests to target auditees (1 day) →Schedule and conduct operational entry meetings with auditees (Within 2 weeks) →Gather, extract, and compile financial and administrative records →Conduct comprehensive field testing and compile formal audit findings →Submit draft reports with remedial recommendations to Management for management responses (By the 10th day of the subsequent month post-quarter) →Aggregate management responses into the final report and present to Council (Within 15 days) →Disseminate approved Quarterly Audit Reports to the Internal Auditor General, Ministry of Education, and MoFPED (Within 30 days post-quarter) →Compile and submit the Annual Consolidated Internal Audit Report to the Internal Auditor General (Within 30 days post-FY) →Conduct quarterly audit follow-ups to verify implementation status of past recommendations and update Council →Coordinate end-of-year exit parameters with the Auditor General/External Auditors.
matrix_rows.coverage: MUBS Main Campus and all Regional Satellite Campuses.
matrix_rows.frequency: Executed continuously; reported Quarterly and Annually.
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: MUBS Council, Top Management, Internal Auditor General, Auditor General, PPDA, IGG, MoES, MoFPED, and Parliament
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Inclusion within the approved annual risk-based audit workplan. -Formally approved Council or ministerial accountability mandate.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: In-person entry/exit meetings, physical asset verification, cash counts, warehouses/stores structural inspections, face-to-face interviews, and hardcopy voucher tracing. Online/Digital: Remote audit portal reviews, electronic ledger transaction analytics, digital extraction of system logs, and secure virtual presentation of findings to Council committees.
matrix_rows.inputs: -Approved Annual Audit Workplan. - Access to institutional financial registries (IFMIS/Navision). - Prior audit reports and pending tracking logs. - National financial regulation manuals. - Internal Audit department technical human resources.

title: Legal Services and Institutional Compliance Standard
pillar: Human Capital, Governance and Institutional Sustainability
supporting_units: Office of the Principal, Office of the School Secretary, Human Resource Directorate, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, All Units
pathway: Request → Registration → Legal Analysis → Consultation → Drafting/Review → Approval → Implementation → Reporting
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for providing legal support, institutional compliance, governance advisory, and dispute resolution services at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all contracts, agreements, and Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) are legally compliant, risk-aware, and aligned with institutional policies.
To provide accurate, evidence-based, confidential, and timely legal opinions.
To manage litigation processes professionally, ensuring timely filings, representation, and resolution of cases.
To ensure that institutional policies are aligned with national laws, regulatory frameworks, and applicable best practices.
To provide effective legal guidance and support to Council and Committee meetings.
To oversee staff and student disciplinary processes in a fair, transparent, and timely manner.
To strengthen legal awareness through structured training and sensitisation programmes.
To ensure secure storage, proper indexing, and easy retrieval of legal records.

matrix_rows.service_description: Drafting and Vetting of Contracts, MoUs, Agreements
performance_indicators: % of contracts vetted within timelines
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Legally compliant, risk-free, aligned with MUBS policies and the Constitution of the Republic of Uganda, 1995, https://www.ngobureau.go.ug/sites/default/files/laws_regulations/2020/12/Uganda%20Constitution%201995.pdf
matrix_rows.process_text: Request submitted (Day 0) → Acknowledgement (Day 1) → Registration & assignment (Day 1) → Preliminary review (Day 1–2) → Legal analysis & compliance check (Day 2–4) → Consultations (Day 3–4) → Final vetted contract issued (Day 5) → Archiving (Within 24 hrs) → Monthly report to Management (By 5th)
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Submission of draft contract
matrix_rows.methodology: Review, compliance check, consultation, approval
matrix_rows.inputs: Draft contract, policies, Acts

performance_indicators: Number of legal opinions issued
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Accurate, evidence-based, confidential.
matrix_rows.process_text: Request submitted (Day 0) → Acknowledgement (Day 1) → Registration & assignment (Day 1) → Legal research & analysis (Day 1–3) → Consultations (Day 2–3) → Opinion drafted & issued (Day 3–5) → Archiving (24 hrs) → Monthly report (By 5th)
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Formal request
matrix_rows.methodology: Legal research, interpretation, advisory memo
matrix_rows.inputs: Statutes, case law

performance_indicators: % of cases responded to within statutory timelines
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Accurate pleadings, timely filings.
matrix_rows.process_text: Receipt of summons/claim (Day 0) → Registration (Day 0–1) → Case assessment (Day 1–3) → Consultations (Day 2–4) → Drafting pleadings (Day 3–7) → Filing (As per court rules) → Court appearances (Ongoing) → Quarterly case status reports
matrix_rows.access_criteria: Existence of legal dispute
matrix_rows.methodology: Case assessment, pleadings, representation
matrix_rows.inputs: Court documents, case files

title: Governance and Secretariat Services Standard
pillar: Human Capital, Governance and Institutional Sustainability
supporting_units: Council Secretariat, Human Resource Directorate, Directorate of Planning, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, All Units, Top Management
pathway: Request → Registration → Agenda Preparation → Consultation → Meeting Facilitation → Documentation → Communication → Implementation Tracking → Reporting
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for providing council secretariat support, governance advisory, and institutional compliance services at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that Council and Committee meetings are properly convened, guided, and documented in accordance with the MUBS Charter and statutory requirements.
To accurately record, communicate, and track the implementation of Council and Committee resolutions.
To provide timely and quality secretariat services, including meeting notices, board packs, minutes, and action reports.
To ensure that institutional policies and decisions are aligned with established governance frameworks and best practices.
To manage staff and student disciplinary processes in a fair, transparent, and timely manner.
To strengthen governance awareness through structured training, sensitisation, and retreat programmes.
To ensure secure storage, proper indexing, and easy retrieval of Council records, minutes, and compliance reports.

matrix_rows.service_description: Provision of Council Secretariat & Governance Services
performance_indicators: i) Total number of statutory Council and specialized committee meetings successfully organized and supported
ii) % of comprehensive boardroom agenda packs compiled and securely dispatched within statutory notice periods
iii) Turnaround time for drafting, validating, and circulating master meeting minutes to members
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% compliance with the MUBS Charter, Universities And Other Tertiary Institutions Act Chapter 262 https://media.ulii.org/media/legislation/128221/source_file/ebbb21906638186d/2001-7.pdf, and approved Council Rules of Procedure.
matrix_rows.process_text: Develop, secure approval for, and publish the Annual Governance Meetings Almanac (At start of FY) →Source, screen, and compile strategic business papers and executive briefs from institutional cost-centres (Ongoing over 3 months) →Edit, index, and securely distribute official meeting notices and comprehensive board packs to members (Exactly 7 calendar days prior to the meeting) →Provide legal and procedural guidance during boardroom sessions and maintain live minutes logs →Formulate, polish, and circulate the first draft of meeting minutes to members for feedback (Within 7 calendar days post-meeting) →Capture and integrate amendments or corrections submitted by members (Within 5 working days of minutes receipt) →Archive signed master minutes into the central institutional registry.
matrix_rows.coverage: Full Main Council and all its statutory sub-committees (e.g., Finance, Appointments, Audit, Development).
matrix_rows.frequency: Conducted continuously; formal statutory sessions held At least 4 times a Year (Quarterly) per committee.
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: Council members Staff management students and other stakeholder
matrix_rows.access_criteria: -Formal gazetting or appointment to the MUBS Council or specialized sub-committees. - Inclusion of active business items on the officially approved meeting agenda.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: Organizing boardroom setups, managing live in-person committee debates, physical security screening of sensitive files, and running governance retreats. Online/Digital: Deployment of specialized encrypted board portal software, digital distribution of board packs, and hosting secure virtual/hybrid governance webinars.
matrix_rows.inputs: -Universities and Other Tertiary Institutions Act (UOTIA) 2001. - Approved MUBS Charter and board rules. -Strategic performance reports from internal university units. -Secure web-based Board Management Portals. -Dedicated Council Secretariat human resources.

matrix_rows.service_description: Monitoring and Tracking of Council Resolutions Implementation
performance_indicators: i)Number of official board resolutions formally extracted, signed, and communicated to executive management for action
ii) % of approved Council directives successfully implemented by Management within agreed timelines
iii) Timeliness of compiling and presenting the master Council Resolutions Action Report
matrix_rows.quality_standard: Absoluteness, accuracy and completeness and traceability in compliance with the MUBS Council Charter 2024
matrix_rows.process_text: Extract and format individual binding resolutions from the raw confirmed minutes of Council sessions (Within 24 hours of meeting closure) → Issue formal Action Directives signed by the Chairperson/Secretary to the Principal to activate executive implementation (Within 1 working day) → Deploy tracking matrices to monitor ongoing operational execution across user units → Collect documentary evidence and field-test compliance claims submitted by Management →Aggregate findings to compile a comprehensive, data-backed Council Resolutions Action Report tracking implementation milestones →Present the Action Report to the Main Council at its subsequent statutory session to highlight progress and performance gaps.
matrix_rows.coverage: Every individual directive, policy mandate, and financial sanction passed by Council across all campuses.
matrix_rows.frequency: Managed continuously on a daily operational basis; consolidated and reported Quarterly.
matrix_rows.target_beneficiary: Top Management, Council Executive Committees, and Institutional Regulatory Auditors
matrix_rows.access_criteria: -Formal passage of a binding resolution during a legally constituted Council or committee session. -Directive assigned to a specific institutional officer for operational execution.
matrix_rows.methodology: Physical: Face-to-face compliance follow-ups with user departments, verification of physical structural projects ordered by Council, and presenting findings to boards. Online/Digital: Maintenance of an automated digital Council Resolutions Tracker Spreadsheet on the intranet, and electronic collection of milestone data from departments.
matrix_rows.inputs: -Confirmed master minutes of Council and committee sessions. -Standardized Resolution Extraction Templates. -Departmental implementation progress logs. -Specialized tracking/project management dashboard software. -Dedicated Quality Assurance and Audit tracking units.
